explanation_after: Dictionaries look up values by key. model_config['heads'] finds the key 'heads' and returns its value: 8. This is O(1) — instant lookup regardless of dictionary size.

stem: A tokenizer maps words to integer IDs. Which data structure best represents this mapping?

options.text: A list — because you can loop through words
A dictionary — maps each word (key) to its ID (value)
A boolean — either the word exists or it doesn't
An integer — IDs are just numbers

explanation_after: Tokenizer vocabulary = {'hello': 7592, 'world': 995, ...}. A dictionary is perfect: O(1) lookup. The GPT-2 tokenizer has 50,257 tokens.

prompt: In Python, a dictionary stores data as ______-______ pairs.
explanation_after: Every dictionary entry has a key (what you look up by) and a value (what you get back). Keys must be unique.
